text.title: TypeScript Core Patterns
text.summary: strict: true options, utility types, generics with constraints, type guards, and Zod validation for runtime safety.

section.title: Strict mode

rule.statement: Enable strict: true plus noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes in tsconfig.json. Never use the any type in production code; use unknown instead.
rule.rationale: strict: true catches null-dereference, untyped params, and unreachable branches at compile time. noUncheckedIndexedAccess makes array[i] return T | undefined, preventing runtime errors.

section.reference: Full tsconfig.json with path aliases and monorepo settings

rule.statement: Prefer named exports over default exports. Use default only for framework requirements (Next.js pages, React.lazy). Named exports enable accurate IDE refactoring and tree-shaking.
rule.rationale: Default exports can be re-imported under any name, making codebase-wide search and automated renaming unreliable.
